hawkish_phrases: the likelihood that elevated inflation could be more persistent had increased
Almost all participants judged that upside risks to the inflation outlook had increased
the Committee was at or near the point at which it would be appropriate to slow the pace of policy easing
uneven progress in returning inflation to 2 percent, the strength of the economy and the labor market
nominal wage growth remained slightly above the pace likely to be consistent over time with 2 percent inflation
dovish_phrases: inflation had made progress toward the Committee's 2 percent objective
such a further lowering of the target range for the policy rate would help maintain the strength in the economy and the labor market
Labor market conditions had generally eased since earlier in the year
there were no signs of rapid deterioration in labor market conditions
summary: The Committee voted 11-1 to cut rates by 25bp to 4.25-4.5%, with one hawkish dissent from Hammack, while signaling a slowdown in the pace of future easing amid increased upside inflation risks and economic strength.
